channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Your AI Wrote a CORS Config That Lets Any Website Read Your API
item.description: TL;DR AI assistants default to Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * — a wildcard that opens your API to every domain on the internet When combined with a naive credentials check, this escalates from...

item.title: Designing an AI That Remembers: How We Built a Coding Mentor with a "Human" Memory
item.description: The Sunday Afternoon Epiphany We’ve all been there. You’re sitting in your room, staring at a LeetCode problem you know you’ve solved before. You remember seeing the "Success" banner two weeks...

item.title: Santa Augmentcode Intent Ep.8
item.description: The Gifts Are Under the Tree — From Spec to Merged PR 🎄 Every year, on the morning of December 25th, I allow myself one quiet moment before the sleigh is unpacked and the Thank-You Letters start...

item.title: How I accidentally built a production tool with Amazon Q and stubbornness
item.description: I am not a developer. I want to be upfront about that. During my bachelor's degree I took a few courses in C, C++ and Java. I understood enough to pass. Later in my career I did some self-study in...

item.title: Spec-Driven Development vs Vibe Coding: My Honest First Impressions After Trying It
item.description: Over the past year, AI-assisted coding has exploded. Tools like Copilot, Claude Code, and GPT-based agents have made it possible to generate large amounts of code simply by describing what you...

item.title: Using Google Antigravity Without Launching Yourself Into Orbit
item.description: There are two ways to use a powerful AI coding agent like Google Antigravity. The first is to walk in with a thoroughly defined plan, tightly scoped instructions, properly framed rules, and...

item.title: Freelance OS Copilot: A Notion MCP-Powered Operations System for Freelancers
item.description: This is a submission for the Notion MCP Challenge Freelance OS Copilot: A Notion MCP-Powered Operations System for Freelancers What I Built I built Freelance OS Copilot, a product that turns a...

item.title: We Audited 20+ Sites for AI Visibility. Here Are the Most Common Mistakes
item.description: Most sites we reviewed had decent technical foundations. Good Core Web Vitals, clean sitemaps, well-structured URLs. They ranked fine in Google. But when you queried their niche in ChatGPT,...

item.title: How to Fortify Cloud Ops Against Geopolitical Attacks 2026
item.description: The world watched the sudden surge of tension between Iran and Israel unfold. Headlines screamed geopolitical drama, but a quieter, more urgent reality unfolded in the clouds: our digital...

item.title: Confronting the CEO of the AI company that impersonated me
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Shishir Mehrotra, who is CEO of Superhuman — that’s the company formerly known as Grammarly, which is still its flagship product. Shishir also used to be the chief product...

item.title: How Excel is Used in Real-World Data Analysis
item.description: Every week, somewhere in the world, a data scientist opens a terminal, fires up Python, and writes elegant code to analyze a dataset. And every week, in even far greater numbers, someone opens...

item.title: The Download: animal welfare gets AGI-pilled, and the White House unveils its AI policy
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The Bay Area’s animal welfare movement wants to recruit AI...

item.title: Every bulb on Govee’s new outdoor string lights can display multicolor effects
item.description: Govee has announced a new set of outdoor string lights that allows for more color customization than competitors' products, as well as the company's existing color-changing set. Similar to its...

item.title: We Found Eight Attack Vectors Inside AWS Bedrock. Here's What Attackers Can Do with Them
item.description: AWS Bedrock is Amazon's platform for building AI-powered applications. It gives developers access to foundation models and the tools to connect those models directly to enterprise data and...

item.title: Microsoft Xbox One Hacked
item.description: It’s an impressive feat, over a decade after the box was released: Since reset glitching wasn’t possible, Gaasedelen thought some voltage glitching could do the trick. So, instead of tinkering...

item.title: Microsoft Warns IRS Phishing Hits 29,000 Users, Deploys RMM Malware
item.description: Microsoft has warned of fresh campaigns that are capitalizing on the upcoming tax season in the U.S. to harvest credentials and deliver malware. The email campaigns take advantage of the urgency...

item.title: Meet the Gods of AI Warfare
item.description: In its early days, the AI initiative known as Project Maven had its fair share of skeptics at the Pentagon. Today, many of them are true believers.

item.title: Samsung adds native AirDrop support to Galaxy S26 devices
item.description: Samsung is bringing AirDrop support to the Quick Share feature on Galaxy devices, starting with the S26 series. The new AirDrop feature will begin rolling out in Korea starting today, according to...

item.title: The Bay Area’s animal welfare movement wants to recruit AI
item.description: In early February, animal welfare advocates and AI researchers gathered in stocking feet at Mox, a scrappy, shoes-free coworking space in San Francisco. Yellow and red canopies billowed overhead,...

item.title: The AI Race Is Pressuring Utilities to Squeeze More From Europe’s Power Grids
item.description: As data center developers queue up to connect to power grids across Europe, network operators are experimenting with novel ways of clearing room for them.

item.title: Trivy Hack Spreads Infostealer via Docker, Triggers Worm and Kubernetes Wiper
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered malicious artifacts distributed via Docker Hub following the Trivy supply chain attack, highlighting the widening blast radius across developer...

item.title: Hackers Exploit CVE-2025-32975 (CVSS 10.0) to Hijack Unpatched Quest KACE SMA Systems
item.description: Threat actors are suspected to be exploiting a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Quest KACE Systems Management Appliance (SMA), according to Arctic Wolf. The cybersecurity company said it...

item.title: AI influencer awards season is upon us
item.description: First came the AI beauty pageant. Then the AI music contests. Now, there is an award for AI Personality of the Year - perhaps the inevitable next step for the AI influencer economy as it fights...

item.title: Topical Dancer is propulsive, playful, and political
item.description: Last week's recommendation, Sotomayor's Wabi Sabi, has a very particular vibe that you don't find in a lot of records. One of the few things it called to mind was 2022's Topical Dancer from...

item.title: Do you want to build a robot snowman?
item.description: On the latest episode of the Equity podcast, we recapped CEO Jensen Huang’s GTC keynote and debated what it means for Nvidia’s future.

item.title: Cursor admits its new coding model was built on top of Moonshot AI’s Kimi
item.description: Building on top of a Chinese model feels particularly fraught right now.

item.title: Crimson Desert dev apologizes for use of AI art
item.description: Reviews of Crimson Desert have been mixed, but the bigger issue for the game has been the discovery of what appeared to be AI-generated assets in the final release. Now the developer has...

item.title: The best deals you can already grab from Amazon’s Big Spring Sale
item.description: Amazon’s Prime Day sale may arrive earlier than usual this year, but there’s a deal event happening much sooner: Amazon’s annual Big Spring Sale, which is happening March 25th through the 31st....

item.title: Elon Musk unveils chip manufacturing plans for SpaceX and Tesla
item.description: Elon Musk recently outlined ambitious plans for a chip-building collaboration Tesla and SpaceX — but he has a history of overpromising.

item.title: Delve accused of misleading customers with ‘fake compliance’
item.description: An anonymous Substack post accuses compliance startup Delve of “falsely” convincing “hundreds of customers they were compliant” with privacy and security regulations.
